Now that we've covered basic file handling, the natural next step is reading files in different formats. This time we'll look at JSON — the format us front-end folks reach for all the time — and see why Python won't let you just dump a dict into a file and call it JSON.

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (18): File Handling
13th iThome Ironman Contest

This time we're going to talk about doing some file handling in Python — things like creating a file and writing data into a specific file. We'll cover open(), the different modes (r, w, a), reading and writing, and at the end the "plus" modes like r+ that let you read and write at the same time.

We've covered a lot of Python's basic syntax in the earlier chapters, so now I want to take a detour and talk about pip. It's something you'll reach for constantly in real-world development. If you already know npm, pip is going to feel pretty familiar — here's a rundown of the commands you'll actually use.

We've wrapped up a chunk of the fundamentals, so now it's time to put those little knowledge points to work. In this one we take the BMI calculator from earlier and add some real features — terminal input, a fake five-second Loading animation, and colored output text in the terminal.

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (13): Reading Input and Printing Output
iThome Ironman 13th

Time for something a little more interactive. We'll look at how you read input from a user in Python with input(), why everything comes back as a string, and then dig into print() — which has more going on than you'd think, including the handy sep and end parameters. It's also setup for the next chapter, because honestly I'm running low on material.

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (10): Container Types, Part 2

Last time we played around with some fun ways to pull values out of a dict. This time I want to keep going with dictionaries — keys, values, items — and then take a detour into tuples, since they show up the moment you call items(). Plus a quick look at Python's in operator and how it compares to JS.

Learning Python From a JS Angle (9) — Container Types, Part 1

How did the BMI homework go? Whether you finished it or not, time waits for no one, so let's move on to the next topic. This time we dig into Python's container types — lists and dictionaries — and how they differ from JavaScript's arrays and objects. We'll also cover the fun stuff like get() and setdefault().

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (8) - BMI Calculator (1)

Time to harvest what we've covered so far with a little exercise. I like to drop a small practice project every few chapters so this series isn't just reading about Python — building something tiny makes it stick. This time it's the classic BMI calculator, written first in JavaScript and then in Python, plus a look at Python's string formatting.

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (6) - Variable Scope
iThome Ironman Contest 13th

Last time we covered functions and variables, so now I want to circle back and fill in something I skipped: variable scope. We'll look at how Python's scoping differs from JavaScript's var/let/const, why Python behaves a lot like var, whether Python has a scope chain, and what nonlocal and global are actually for.

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (4) - Types and Variables

Okay, we're finally writing some actual Python. We've messed around for three days now, and if I don't start soon people are going to unsubscribe. Before we write anything, we kick off with the most basic thing there is: types. And as always, I'll compare Python and JavaScript side by side so the differences are easy to spot.
